entities.name: Perovskite-Silicon Tandem Cell
entities.description: Stacking a perovskite top cell (wide bandgap) on a silicon bottom cell captures more of the solar spectrum. This is the fastest path to exceeding the silicon single-junction limit (29.4%).
entities.developer: Oxford PV, LONGi, Hanwha Qcells, CubicPV
entities.status: Oxford PV shipping first commercial tandem modules (2024); mass production 2025-2026

entities.name: All-Perovskite Tandem Cell
entities.description: Two perovskite layers with different bandgaps stacked together. Potentially cheaper than perovskite-silicon tandems since both layers are solution-processable. Key challenge: stable narrow-bandgap perovskite (tin-lead).
entities.status: Lab efficiency records climbing rapidly; stability improving; commercialization 5+ years

entities.name: HJT (Heterojunction Technology)
entities.efficiency: 26.81% (LONGi, world record for silicon single junction)
entities.description: Combines crystalline silicon with amorphous silicon layers for high efficiency and low temperature coefficient. Better performance in hot climates than PERC.
entities.status: Commercial; growing market share; LONGi's 26.81% broke the silicon efficiency record

entities.name: TOPCon (Tunnel Oxide Passivated Contact)
entities.description: The current mainstream upgrade from PERC, adding a tunnel oxide layer for better surface passivation. Dominating new capacity additions globally.
entities.developer: Jinko Solar, Trina, JA Solar, LONGi
entities.status: Mainstream; >60% of new capacity in 2024; lowest $/W in mass production

entities.name: CIGS Thin Film
entities.description: Copper indium gallium selenide thin-film solar cells. Flexible, lightweight, and good performance in low-light and high-temperature conditions. Used in BIPV and portable applications.
entities.developer: Solar Frontier, Avancis, Manz AG, Flisom
entities.status: Niche market; flexible and BIPV applications; indium supply concern

entities.name: Organic Photovoltaics (OPV)
entities.description: Carbon-based semiconductors enabling ultra-lightweight, flexible, semi-transparent solar cells. Can be printed on plastic films. Very low cost potential but limited efficiency and stability.
entities.status: Niche applications (BIPV, IoT power); efficiency gap too large for mainstream

entities.name: Concentrated Solar Power (CSP) with Thermal Storage
entities.description: Mirrors concentrate sunlight to heat molten salt, which stores energy for 10+ hours of dispatchable power. Provides baseload solar without batteries.
entities.status: Operational plants in Morocco, UAE, China, Spain; declining costs but still expensive vs PV+battery

entities.name: Bifacial Solar Modules
entities.efficiency: 5-25% more energy than monofacial depending on albedo
entities.description: Modules that capture light from both sides, using ground-reflected light to boost output. Now standard for utility-scale installations.

entities.name: Lithium Iron Phosphate (LFP) Battery
entities.description: The dominant battery chemistry for grid storage and EVs (especially in China). No cobalt or nickel, lower cost, longer cycle life (3,000-10,000 cycles), but lower energy density than NMC.

entities.name: Sodium-Ion Battery
entities.description: Uses abundant sodium instead of lithium. Lower energy density but excellent low-temperature performance and zero lithium dependency. CATL and HiNa are leading commercialization.
entities.developer: CATL, HiNa Battery, Faradion, Natron Energy

entities.name: Solid-State Battery
entities.description: Replaces liquid electrolyte with solid ceramic or polymer, enabling lithium metal anodes for 2x energy density and improved safety. The holy grail of battery technology.
entities.developer: Toyota, QuantumScape, Solid Power, Samsung SDI, SES AI

entities.name: Iron-Air Battery
entities.description: Uses iron oxidation (rusting) for ultra-cheap, long-duration energy storage. Target cost: <$20/kWh. Ideal for multi-day grid storage. Form Energy's first plant is building 10MW/1GWh system for Xcel Energy.

entities.name: Redox Flow Battery (Vanadium)
entities.description: Stores energy in liquid electrolyte tanks. Power and energy scale independently. Vanadium flow batteries offer 20,000+ cycles with no degradation. Ideal for long-duration grid storage.

entities.name: Pumped Hydro Storage
entities.description: The most mature and widely deployed grid storage technology (95% of global storage capacity). Pumps water uphill when excess power is available, releases through turbines when needed. China leads with 50+ GW installed.
entities.developer: Various utilities; China Three Gorges, Voith, Andritz

entities.name: Compressed Air Energy Storage (CAES)
entities.description: Compresses air into underground caverns or tanks. Advanced adiabatic CAES captures heat during compression for higher efficiency. Hydrostor is building 500MW projects in Australia and California.

entities.name: Gravity Energy Storage
entities.description: Raises heavy blocks or masses using excess electricity, then lowers them to generate power. Energy Vault's EVx uses composite blocks in a tower. Simple, long-life, no degradation.

entities.name: Small Modular Reactor (SMR) Integration
entities.description: SMRs providing baseload power that complements variable renewables. SMRs can load-follow and provide process heat for industrial applications.
entities.integration: SMR + solar/wind + storage; district heating; hydrogen production
entities.status: GE Hitachi BWRX-300 under construction in Canada; Rolls-Royce SMR in UK

entities.name: Nuclear Hydrogen Production
entities.description: Using nuclear heat and electricity for high-temperature electrolysis or thermochemical hydrogen production. More efficient than using variable renewables alone.
entities.integration: HTGR/SMR + SOEC electrolysis; thermochemical cycles (S-I, Cu-Cl)
entities.status: DOE hydrogen hubs; pilot projects at Idaho National Lab; Japan HTTR
